title: Unter den Linden Berlin - Quick Summary
items: **Unter den Linden Berlin is the classic east-west walk** between Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island, with the Humboldt Forum at the eastern end.
**For a first visit, walk it slowly once, not as a commute.** The useful stops are Brandenburg Gate, Bebelplatz, Humboldt University, Neue Wache, Zeughaus, Schlossbruecke and Museum Island.
**The full boulevard is about 1.5 km,** but the better tourist plan is 60 to 90 minutes with pauses, photos and one or two side stops.
**Use U5 if you need a shortcut:** Brandenburger Tor, Unter den Linden and Museumsinsel stations let you start, split or rescue the walk without losing the main line.
**The mistake is treating it as only pretty architecture.** The boulevard works best when you read it as Prussian power, Nazi rupture, GDR rebuilding and reunited Berlin on one street.
